response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web-focused engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js credentials and broad experience across platforms, but he lacks virtually every domain-specific requirement for this role. The most disqualifying gap is the absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a hard requirement. Beyond that, there is no demonstrated experience with embedded networking (LAN switches, MACsec, 802.1X), no Linux kernel work, and his C programming appears limited to isolated academic and early-career projects rather than sustained production-level embedded development. His background is primarily web, cloud, and application-layer work, which does not transfer to this firmware/embedded security role.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across multiple languages and platforms, demonstrating strong general adaptability
Some early C/C++ exposure (National Instruments, Sound Position Server thesis) and self-described familiarity with embedded systems
Fluent English satisfies the documentation and email communication requirement
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a stated hard requirement that alone disqualifies the candidate
Zero experience with embedded networking security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, Layer-2/3 switching, SNMP, NETCONF)
No evidence of Linux kernel investigation or source-code modification; C programming is minimal and non-embedded in nature
